decision: Add thin input handling and explicit CSV logging to skill
context: INK-001 dogfood revealed edge cases not covered by input contract
evidence: OBS-001: thin input bypassed Phase 1; CSV logging emerged autonomously
learning: Output contracts get interpreted literally — emergent behaviors that work should be codified explicitly
next_steps: Continue PRE-001 validation with 4 more test artifacts

decision: Acknowledge dual-purpose tension in Big Mad Study design
context: Study serves two functions: research validity AND positioning artifact for Good Citizens. Screener machine-speed question (Q: expect people to act as fast as machines?) primes participants before journaling.
evidence: Outreach script to [DL-001] positions study as credibility signal. Screener integrity check (Have you helped design this study?) is genuine bias control — a design decision not a gap.
learning: Artifacts serving dual purposes create validity/narrative entanglement. Intake must distinguish design gaps from design decisions. Priming effects from screeners are validity threats to name not flaws to fix.
next_steps: Log priming as study limitation. Screener journals treated as framed not unprompted. Generate PRE-002 for cohort validity.

decision: Close INK-002 and INK-003 as linked artifacts
context: The Flattening manifesto (INK-003) is the worldview underneath [DL-001] outreach (INK-002). Intake treated them as independent — they're not. The car in INK-003 is a physical hypothesis with a real falsification gate (H1: sells at premium with story attached).
evidence: OBS-002: Manifesto converts emotionally but leaves commercial close implicit. The gap between values and livelihood is unstated. H1's stopping condition (KBB value) is stronger evidence than any pitch deck slide.
learning: Artifact sequencing matters. Intakes should ask: who is the named recipient? Is this artifact part of a positioning sequence? Most manifestos never produce a measurable signal — this one has one built in.
next_steps: PRE-001 continues. 4/5 artifacts tested. 1 remaining to reach threshold.

decision: Flag correlation-without-control as hypothesis flaw; prioritize failure criteria gap
context: INK-004 (own work) is HDD-compliant but H8 uses 'correlated' in success metric. Test method doesn't control for confounds (call volume, lead quality, time of month, MSAM coaching). Gap equivalence error in initial Copilot intake — gaps were listed as if equivalent. They're not.
evidence: OBS-003: Standard Lean UX template stops at 'we will know we are successful when' — no 'we will abandon when' clause. The Kit's stopping condition requirement is the correction. Correlation claims in test methods need controlled experiment design to produce defensible signal.
learning: Three learnings: (1) Failure criteria gap is more important than prioritization or sequencing gaps. (2) 'Correlated' in a success metric is a flag for uncontrolled observation. (3) Own-work artifacts require same rigor but different intake posture — methodology vs deliverable distinction matters for IP.
next_steps: Update skill to flag correlation-as-causation in hypothesis review. Add failure criteria warning to Phase 4. PRE-001 at 4/5.

decision: Close PRE-001 as validated; open PRE-003 for experiment design validity
context: 5/5 artifacts tested. Threshold met. Every artifact produced more specific testable output than existed before. Intake surfaced gaps invisible to original authors.
evidence: OBS-001 through OBS-004: thin input handling, artifact sequencing, correlation/causation flaw, detection vs prediction distinction. Five learnings, three unpatched.
learning: Skill's weakest phase is Phase 5 output routing. Input contract catches structural gaps; output contract doesn't evaluate experiment design quality. A well-formed hypothesis with a poorly designed test method passes. It shouldn't.
next_steps: (1) Patch linked artifact sequencing check in input contract. (2) Add experiment design validity check to Phase 5. (3) Add DETECTION vs PREDICTION artifact classification. Register PRE-003.
